refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: supporting teleoperation with communication quality that meets a specified packet loss limit. It does not combine multiple requirements or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to ensure packet loss stays below threshold
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
Interaction between retransmission logic and teleoperation control loop
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality management within the protocol layer, minimizing coupling with other system functions. Adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms can be designed modularly to meet packet loss targets independently.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and do not degrade unexpectedly
Risk that adaptive mechanisms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes error correction and retransmission can be tuned to meet both packet loss and latency requirements
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency constraints alongside packet loss in the FR to ensure the DP addresses all teleoperation quality aspects.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network communication stack and protocol implementations responsible for managing data transmission, including error correction and retransmission mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network layer for sending and receiving packets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send status and performance data related to communication quality and error correction metrics.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol logically belongs here; minimal diffusion as error correction is core to network communication.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not implement protocol; would cause high diffusion if protocol implemented here.
Teleoperation depends on communication but is not responsible for protocol implementation; poor containment.
Safety Services may monitor communication but should not implement protocol; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission, as it directly manages network communication functions and can encapsulate the protocol logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
If error correction parameters need to be adjusted dynamically by other modules, interface complexity may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, fulfilling the functional requirement for teleoperation communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
If error correction parameters need to be adjusted dynamically by other modules, interface complexity may increase.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within a single IM boundary. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface, ensuring packet loss remains ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the communication protocol DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined external interfaces for network communication and telemetry.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document interfaces to lower-level hardware to ensure full encapsulation within NGHE Solution and reduce dependency risks.
Define protocols or mechanisms for dynamic adjustment of error correction parameters by other modules to manage interface complexity and maintain modularity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justifications and acceptable risk levels. The DP directly addresses the FR with a well-defined communication protocol, and the DP is well encapsulated within a single IM boundary (NGHE Solution) with high containment and low diffusion. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although some minor risks related to hardware interface dependencies and dynamic parameter adjustments exist, these are manageable through recommended revisions and do not warrant further decomposition at this stage, especially given the high safety criticality which favors stability and clarity over deeper decomposition.

revise.why: To clarify and document interfaces to lower-level hardware to ensure full encapsulation within NGHE Solution and to define protocols for dynamic adjustment of error correction parameters, thereby reducing dependency risks and managing interface complexity.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently captures the functional requirement for teleoperation communication quality.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for the DP.
Verification through telemetry monitoring is reliable and feasible under agreed network conditions.
open_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not fully encapsulated within NGHE Solution.
Interface complexity may increase if error correction parameters need dynamic adjustment by other modules.
evidence_needed: Documentation of hardware interface encapsulation within NGHE Solution.
Defined protocols or mechanisms for dynamic adjustment of error correction parameters.
Verification data confirming packet loss ≤1% under operational conditions.
